name: Spaghetti Carbonara
instructions:
1. Bring a large pot of salted water to boil and cook spaghetti al dente.
2. Fry pancetta in a pan until crispy. Remove from heat.
3. Beat eggs with grated pecorino and plenty of black pepper.
4. Drain pasta, reserving a cup of pasta water.
5. Quickly toss hot pasta with pancetta (off heat), add egg mixture, stirring fast.
6. Add pasta water a splash at a time to reach a creamy consistency.
7. Serve immediately with extra cheese and pepper.

name: Greek Salad
instructions:
1. Chop tomatoes and cucumber into large chunks.
2. Thinly slice the red onion.
3. Combine vegetables in a bowl with olives.
4. Top with a block (or crumbled) feta cheese.
5. Drizzle generously with olive oil.
6. Sprinkle dried oregano and a pinch of salt.
7. Toss gently and serve immediately.

name: Chocolate Lava Cake
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 220°C. Grease ramekins with butter and dust with cocoa.
2. Melt chocolate and butter together.
3. Whisk eggs, yolks, and sugar until pale.
4. Fold chocolate mixture into eggs, then fold in flour.
5. Pour into ramekins and refrigerate 30 minutes (or up to 24h).
6. Bake 12 minutes — edges set, centre still wobbly.
7. Rest 1 minute, then invert onto a plate. Serve with ice cream.

name: Avocado Toast
instructions:
1. Toast sourdough slices until golden and crisp.
2. Halve and pit avocados. Scoop flesh into a bowl.
3. Mash with a fork; add lemon juice, salt and olive oil to taste.
4. Spread generously over toast.
5. Top with chili flakes and a drizzle of olive oil.
6. Optional: add a poached egg, smoked salmon, or microgreens.

name: Chicken Tikka Masala
instructions:
1. Marinate chicken in yogurt, lemon and spices for at least 1 hour.
2. Grill or pan-fry chicken until charred. Set aside.
3. Sauté onion, garlic and ginger until golden.
4. Add tomatoes and spices; simmer 15 minutes.
5. Blend sauce smooth, return to pan.
6. Add chicken and cream; simmer 10 minutes.
7. Garnish with fresh coriander and serve with rice or naan.

name: Panzanella
instructions:
1. Tear bread into chunks; lightly toast or leave stale overnight.
2. Soak bread in water 10 minutes, then squeeze dry.
3. Chop tomatoes and cucumber; slice onion thin.
4. Combine all vegetables with capers and bread.
5. Dress with red wine vinegar and plenty of olive oil.
6. Add torn basil leaves. Season to taste.
7. Rest 30 minutes before serving — flavours improve.
